scenario_results.reason: Assessment stayed bounded, requested missing discovery evidence, and prioritized first-wave analysis.
Pathway selection used explicit tradeoffs and avoided implementation-level prescriptions.
Batch and scheduler dependencies were reflected in wave sequencing, failure windows, and rollback gating.
Coexistence plan covered facade and transition patterns while routing build work to adjacent specialists.
Cutover guidance ranked risks, defined validation gates, and documented rollback triggers.
Closed-platform constraints were handled with realistic planning options and explicit uncertainty.
Operating-model risk and staffing gaps were incorporated without widening into org-design ownership.
Roadmap sequencing balanced value, effort, and modernization risk with clear tradeoffs.
Greenfield platform design was rejected clearly as outside the specialty boundary.
Scheduler and middleware operations requests were routed away while preserving planning context.
Low-confidence handling requested missing evidence and escalated rather than guessing.
Fallback behavior used the local authoritative baseline and stated freshness limits explicitly.

notes: Scenario suite covers normal planning work, legacy constraint handling, integration and automation failure modes, migration-risk prioritization, out-of-scope rejection, and low-confidence escalation.
No unreviewed regressions remain against the issue #147 acceptance threshold.
